body.u: “Looking back on when I left school, it was like turning into a new world. A much bigger one. It was a most frightening experience at first, but then I got used to the idea, then it became quite exciting. I soon found myself a full-time job in a factory as a seamstress for “Race furniture”. I was proud of my very first wage of £19.20p even if it wasn’t as much as what I expected.

Things started happening very fast after that. In November nineteen seventy eight I got engaged to a most wonderful person. We had been courting for two years. In the summer of nineteen eighty we were wed. It was a most wonderful wedding, and the weather was fine.

In eighty three “John”, our first son came along. One and a half year later “Jayne” arrived. We all travelled around the world together. By now I was only part-time at “Race Furniture”, as I spent most of my time tidying up after the children and picking them up from school.
John was married at the age of eighteen.
She is a delightful girl his wife. Very shy though. Has a heart of gold. Shortly after Jayne was married. Sad really, for she couldn’t have any children, so they adopted a little boy.
At the age of sixty one my husband died. How I miss him. There’s only my here now. John and his wife and three kids live in Australia, and Jayne is divorced living in London with her son.
But, at least I still have my memories with me….”
